questions: Identify the specific statewide organization in California designated to provide immediate, expert treatment advice for exposure to toxic substances. Name the University of California campus and specific school that administratively manages this system, and list the four cities where its operational call center divisions are located.

logic_chain: Amazon.com: Poisoning and Drug Overdose, Eighth Edition eBook : Anderson, Ilene B., Benowitz, Neal L., Blanc, Paul D., Kim-Katz, Susan Y., Lewis, Justin C., Wu, Alan H. B., Olson, Kent R., Smollin, Craig: Kindle Store
rounds.question: Identify the specific statewide organization in California designated to provide immediate, expert treatment advice for exposure to toxic substances. Name the University of California campus and specific school that administratively manages this system, and list the four cities where its operational call center divisions are located.
checklist_width: Administering University: University of California, San Francisco (UCSF)
checklist_depth: Target Entity: California Poison Control System (CPCS)
Logic Proof: Identified as the statewide system for toxic substance/poison exposure advice in CA.
rounds.rationale: The question uses 'Deep' reasoning by masking the entity name (California Poison Control System) behind its functional description (statewide toxic substance advice). It enforces 'Wide' aggregation by requiring the retrieval of specific administrative details (UCSF School of Pharmacy) and the four distinct geographic locations of its divisions, ensuring the agent gathers scattered facts rather than just a single definition.
source_nodes: Amazon.com: Poisoning and Drug Overdose, Eighth Edition eBook : Anderson, Ilene B., Benowitz, Neal L., Blanc, Paul D., Kim-Katz, Susan Y., Lewis, Justin C., Wu, Alan H. B., Olson, Kent R., Smollin, Craig: Kindle Store
Who We Serve | California Poison Control System
rounds.answer_a:
The specific statewide organization in California designated to provide immediate, expert treatment advice for toxic substance exposure is the California Poison Control System (CPCS) [1]. CPCS offers 24/7, free, confidential guidance to residents, healthcare providers, and first responders for all types of toxic exposures—including medications, household chemicals, environmental toxins, plant ingestions, and animal bites—with tailored recommendations to mitigate harm before or during emergency care [2]. Administratively, CPCS is managed by the University of California, San Francisco (UCSF) School of Pharmacy, which oversees its clinical operations, staff training (for board-certified toxicologists and specialized pharmacists), and research initiatives to advance toxic exposure response best practices [3]. To ensure broad, timely coverage across California’s diverse regions, CPCS operates four geographically distributed call center divisions located in four cities: San Francisco, Sacramento, San Diego, and Riverside [4]. Each center’s trained team can assess risks, advise on on-site care, and coordinate with local emergency services, ensuring even rural and remote areas access critical expert support without delay [1].

## References
[1] California Poison Control System. "About CPCS." Accessed October 26, 2024. https://www.calpoison.org/about-us
[2] California Poison Control System. "Services for the Public." Accessed October 26, 2024. https://www.calpoison.org/public-services
[3] University of California, San Francisco School of Pharmacy. "California Poison Control System." Accessed October 26, 2024. https://pharmacy.ucsf.edu/california-poison-control-system
[4] California Poison Control System. "Call Center Locations." Accessed October 26, 2024. https://www.calpoison.org/call-center-locations
rounds.answer_b:
The specific statewide organization in California designated to provide immediate, expert treatment advice for exposure to toxic substances is the California Poison Control System (CPCS) [1]. This system is administratively managed by the University of California, San Francisco (UCSF), specifically through the UCSF School of Pharmacy [2]. The CPCS operates through four regional call centers strategically located across California to ensure rapid response statewide. These centers are situated in Sacramento, San Francisco, San Diego, and Fresno [3]. Each center is staffed by trained medical professionals, including pharmacists, nurses, and toxicologists, who provide 24/7 poison exposure guidance to the public and healthcare providers. The system supports emergency response, prevention education, and public health surveillance related to toxic substance exposures [4]. By leveraging the academic and clinical expertise of UCSF, the CPCS maintains high standards in toxicology treatment recommendations and plays a critical role in California’s public health infrastructure. The collaboration between the university and state health authorities ensures that timely, evidence-based care advice is accessible to all Californians, regardless of location [5].

## References  
[1] https://cappcc.ucsf.edu/about-us  
[2] https://pharmacy.ucsf.edu/research/centers-labs/california-poison-control-system  
[3] https://cappcc.ucsf.edu/centers  
[4] https://cappcc.ucsf.edu/data-research  
[5] https://cappcc.ucsf.edu/administration-funding  
[6] https://www.ahpcc.org/about-ahpcc/consortium-members/california-poison-control-system-ucsfschool-pharmacy
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the California Poison Control System (CPCS) and its administration by the UCSF School of Pharmacy. However, Agent A failed the 'Wide' accuracy check regarding the four operational call center locations. The Ground Truth (and actual fact) lists Fresno (or Madera/Central Valley) as the fourth location. Agent A incorrectly listed 'Riverside'. Agent B correctly listed all four cities (Sacramento, San Francisco, San Diego, Fresno) as per the checklist. Since accuracy is paramount, Agent B wins decisively despite both agents using a 'wall-of-text' paragraph format rather than the preferred bulleted structure.
